content:
8 min read
Just now

--

If you’ve spent more than a month in Salesforce development, you’ve heard it a thousand times: bulkify your triggers. It’s true, it’s necessary, and it’s also where most tutorials stop — right at the point where the real work begins.
Bulkification tells you not to put SOQL or DML inside a loop. It doesn’t tell you why your batch job is still timing out at 40,000 records, why your heap blows up on record #6,000 instead of record #1, or how to decouple a process so it never hits a limit at all. That’s the gap this article is for.
We’ll go through three things most “bulkify your code” posts skip entirely:

• How to actually profile Apex execution instead of guessing where the bottleneck is

• How to read and reason about heap consumption, with a worked example of a heap blowup and its fix

• How to use Queueable chaining and Platform Events to decouple processing so governor limits stop being a ceiling
By the end, you’ll have a working example that processes a large batch of records without breaking a sweat — and the diagnostic skills to find the next bottleneck yourself.
1. Profiling Apex: Stop Guessing, Start Measuring
Most developers treat governor limits like a black box: the job fails, they sprinkle in some System.debug() calls, shrug, and add a LIMIT clause somewhere. Senior…
